commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds fence-aware Real behavior proof section slicing and a focused regression test for headings inside copied fenced terminal output.

PR surface: Tests +30, Other +18. Total +48 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has a clear source-level section-slicing bug, and the proposed helper can be probed to still stop at a fenced `##` heading after a same-length info-string fence line.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update the fence-close predicate and add the missing regression without a product decision.

target.title: fix(ci): ignore fenced headings in proof parser
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens the Real behavior proof parser so Markdown-looking headings inside fenced terminal output do not end the proof section early.

This keeps the existing proof field requirements and mock-only checks intact; the only change is that section slicing now ignores headings while inside backtick or tilde fences. The helper now closes a fence only when the closing marker uses the same character and is at least as long as the opening marker.

## Context

PR #87341 exposed the bug: its real behavior proof copied a generated prompt that contained `## TOOLS.md` inside a fenced output block. The current parser treated that line as the next PR-body heading and missed the later `Observed result after fix` / `What was not tested` fields.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Real behavior proof sections keep parseable fields after copied terminal output that contains Markdown headings such as `## TOOLS.md`, including when a longer outer fence contains a shorter inner fence line.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/proof-parser-fence-aware-section`, commit `3e44817611b536ea1770295eaf04de2b9dd91846`, Node 24-compatible runtime.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a direct local node smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs` with a synthetic external PR body whose four-backtick fenced terminal output contains an inner triple-backtick line and `## TOOLS.md`, followed by `Observed result after fix` and `What was not tested`. Also ran the focused policy test file with the repo Vitest wrapper.

Evidence after fix: The direct node smoke returned `section contains inner triple fence: true`, `section contains fenced heading: true`, `status: passed`, and preserved both required fields after the fenced output. Focused regression tests also passed 1 file / 27 tests. Copied output is included below.

Observed result after fix: `extractRealBehaviorProofSections` preserves the fenced inner triple-backtick line and `## TOOLS.md` heading as proof-section content, and `evaluateRealBehaviorProof` still returns `passed` while reading the required fields that appear after the fenced output.

What was not tested: Live GitHub Actions proof-gate rerun after this force-push. `pnpm test test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts` was attempted locally before the first push but pnpm stopped before tests with a dependency purge/install prompt, so the repo node wrapper was used instead.

Direct parser smoke output:

```text
section contains inner triple fence: true
section contains fenced heading: true
status: passed
observedResult: Parser preserved the fenced heading and still read fields below the terminal transcript.
notTested: Live GitHub Actions rerun before opening this draft PR.
```

## Verification

- `node --input-type=module` direct parser smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Refs #87341
target.author: monkeyleet
